description_long: This is a pitched-roof canopy structure supported by four sturdy wooden posts, each anchored into short brick columns at the base. The roof is covered with dark gray shingles arranged in neat rows, providing effective shelter from above. The wooden frame features visible trusses and beams, adding both structural integrity and a decorative touch. The brick bases are constructed with a classic red-brown brick pattern, giving the structure a robust and permanent appearance. The open sides allow for easy passage beneath the canopy, making it suitable for use as a shelter at entrances, walkways, or small gathering areas. The overall design is utilitarian yet aesthetically pleasing, blending natural wood, masonry, and modern roofing materials.
description: A pitched-roof canopy with a shingled roof, wooden frame, and brick base supports, open on all sides for easy passage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched roof, wooden trusses, and two brick bases with open entry beneath the canopy.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the shingled roof and the solid brick base, with the open side visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the wooden trusses and brick supports from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the roof and brick base, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_support_structure_design: The canopy is supported by four vertical wooden posts, each embedded into a short, rectangular brick column. The posts are connected at the top by horizontal beams and reinforced with diagonal trusses, forming a stable frame beneath the pitched roof.
attribute_edge_detailing: The roof edges are straight and clean, with exposed wooden beams and a slight overhang for water runoff. No decorative trim or scalloping is present.
